name: Abhimanyu Timbadia
role: Strategy, Founder’s Office at Alt Carbon
html: Abhimanyu worked on PUCAR through Samagra, where he spent two years on governance programmes ranging from courtroom processes to open-source communities and agricultural systems serving over 90 lakh farmers. He has since joined the founder's office at Alt Carbon, a carbon removal startup, and is starting his MBA at IIM Bangalore. A gold medalist from Ashoka University, he writes regularly on technology and consumer culture.

name: Abhishek Rao
html: Abhishek is a Senior Consultant at Samagra, where he has spent close to five years working with national and state governments on complex service delivery problems. He currently leads Samagra's engagement with the Ministry of Health's national HIV/AIDS programme, running across 18 states and 219 districts. He contributes to PUCAR's people-centric courts work through Samagra's justice portfolio.

name: Aditi Phadnis
html: Aditi is the Product Lead at PUCAR, driving DRISTI, the open-source dispute resolution platform at the heart of the collective's work. Before this she built population-scale public systems at Samagra and worked at J.P. Morgan India. She holds an MBA from the University of Oxford.

name: Aishwariya Dixit
html: Aishwariya works with Deloitte, supporting consulting engagements connected to PUCAR's ecosystem.

name: Ajay Nandalike
html: Ajay is a Partner at Pragati Law Chambers in Bengaluru and Of Counsel at Dharmaprabhas Law Associates in New Delhi. A disputes lawyer for nearly two decades, he began in the dispute resolution practice at Dua Associates before building his own practice. He brings a practitioner's view of how litigation actually unfolds to PUCAR's court transformation work.

name: Akshay Roongta
html: Akshay is the co-founder of Dots, an Ooloi Labs spin-off that turns scattered user feedback into structured, traceable insights so decisions rest on evidence rather than guesswork. He co-founded Ooloi Labs in 2019 and has spent years building tools for how collectives work together. Off screen he thinks about qualitative data, amateur birding, and books.

name: Anshumanth Rao
html: Anshumanth is a Product Manager at PUCAR, having moved over from eGov Foundation where he worked on the collective's dispute resolution tooling as an Associate Product Manager. Before turning to public technology he was an analyst at Goldman Sachs and a software engineer at Cisco. An ISB Dean's List graduate, he is also a published author.

name: Ashok Pal Singh
html: Ashok Pal Singh is a former Government of India officer who now advises boards on government affairs and public systems reform. His advisory work spans financial services, digital ID, health, child rights, and postal reform, with engagements including the World Bank, Rang De, and Salaam Baalak Trust. He brings decades of experience navigating the machinery of the Indian state.

name: Atul Gupta
role: CTO, PUCAR and Innovator in Residence, Agami
html: Atul is PUCAR's Chief Technology Officer and an Innovator in Residence at Agami, leading the technology strategy behind DRISTI, the open, modular and interoperable platform powering the collective's court transformation work. A cloud architect by training, he has spent years designing digital public infrastructure and digital public goods for national scale. He is also a certified independent director and ESG expert.

name: Bharat Chugh
role: Advocate and Founder, The Chambers of Bharat Chugh
html: Bharat is a former judge of the Delhi Judicial Service who now practises as arguing counsel, leading a team of over a dozen lawyers at The Chambers of Bharat Chugh in Delhi and Gurgaon. He was earlier a Partner at L&L Partners and is a Registered Foreign Lawyer at the Singapore International Commercial Court. He lectures widely, from the National Police Academy to judicial academies, and writes on justice and law.

name: Bhargavi Zaveri-Shah
html: Bhargavi is the co-founder and CEO of The Professeer and works at the intersection of litigation analytics, finance, and governance. She spent years as a researcher with XKDR Forum studying how India's courts actually perform, helping build the evidence base for judicial reform. Her work brings data rigour to questions the legal system has long answered by anecdote.

name: Bhuvanyu Guru
html: Bhuvanyu is a software development engineer in test at BeeHyv, building end-to-end test automation for web and mobile platforms with Playwright, JMeter, and API tooling. He supports PUCAR's DRISTI platform as an L2 engineer, helping keep court technology dependable.

name: Chittu Nagarajan
role: Founder, CREK ODR and Co-founder, ODR.com
html: Chittu is one of the pioneers of online dispute resolution: founder of CREK ODR, co-founder of ODR.com (acquired by the American Arbitration Association), and co-creator of ODR LAB. She has advised UN Women on gender-responsive legal technology, supporting access to justice programmes across Nepal, Timor-Leste, Fiji, Solomon Islands, and Indonesia. She works from Chennai.

name: Deepika Kinhal
html: Deepika is the Chief Executive Officer of CORD and a leading voice in India's legal-tech and online dispute resolution space. She spent over seven years at the Vidhi Centre for Legal Policy, where she led the JALDI mission on access to justice and lowering delays in India. Her current work spans ODR, AI for disputes, and court technology.

name: Deeshita Soin
html: Deeshita is a Senior Associate at Samagra, which she joined in 2023 after Ashoka University. At Samagra she works on governance transformation programmes, including the justice portfolio that supports PUCAR's people-centric courts work. Earlier she managed portfolios at Bodhi Capital and led operations for the Ashoka Investments Club.

name: Devaraj D
html: Devaraj is a Product Manager at IBM in Kochi, building low-code, real-time event processing experiences on Apache Flink. Before IBM he was a Product Manager at Samagra, where he contributed to PUCAR's dispute resolution work. He holds degrees from XLRI Jamshedpur and CET Trivandrum.

name: Gaurav Goel
html: Gaurav is the founder and CEO of Samagra, the governance consulting firm he started in 2012 that works with political and bureaucratic leadership at the Centre and in states to solve governance problems at scale. Samagra pairs a top-down consulting approach with bottom-up understanding of the governance ecosystem, backed by deep technology and data expertise. Its justice portfolio anchors much of PUCAR's court transformation work.

name: Jeevika Shiv
role: Thematic Lead, Feminist Movements and Leadership, UN Women
html: Jeevika works with UN Women headquarters as Thematic Coordination Lead for Feminist Movements and Leadership under Generation Equality. A lawyer by training, she has spent six years with UN Women on gender equality, leadership, and systems strategy. She is also a facilitator and group analyst in training, working at the intersection of human rights and systems dynamics.

name: Kailash Nadh
html: Kailash is the Chief Technology Officer of Zerodha, India's largest stock brokerage, where famously small engineering teams run systems at national scale. He is also the founder of Samagata Foundation, which supports public commons projects across science, culture, technology, and research, and co-founder of the Indic Digital Archive Foundation and FLOSS/fund. He brings a builder's scepticism and an open-source ethos to PUCAR's technology thinking.

name: Kanchan Gupta
html: Kanchan is the co-founder of CADRE (Centre for Alternate Dispute Resolution Excellence), where he has spent seven years building what he calls a dispute resolution operating system. He came to ODR from digital transformation roles at Max Life Insurance, and has taught as visiting faculty at the National Law School of India University.

name: Keerthana Medarametla
html: Keerthana spent over seven years at Agami, most recently as Senior Curator, discovering and connecting the people and ideas reimagining law and justice in India. She was earlier a Senior Research Fellow at NLU Delhi's Centre for Constitutional Law, Policy and Governance. She is one of the threads that ties the PUCAR collective together.

name: Kesava Moorthy
html: Kesava is the co-founder and CTO of ADReS Now (ADR e-Sarvatra), an online dispute resolution venture he started in 2019. Before that he spent 18 years at Wipro, rising to Vice President and Delivery Head for banking, financial services, energy and utilities. He brings enterprise-scale delivery experience to justice technology.

name: Kunal Ahirwar
html: Kunal heads product at Finarkein Analytics, building products that help financial institutions use consented financial data across lending, monitoring, and collections. He was earlier Product Lead at Sahamati, the collective driving India's account aggregator and open banking ecosystem. His work sits squarely in the world of digital public infrastructure and open finance.

name: Madhav Pudipeddi
html: Madhav is associated with JustAct, the online dispute resolution platform from Lex Carta.

name: Manimaaran Sooriamoorthy
html: Manimaaran is a Tech Lead supporting the engineering behind PUCAR's DRISTI platform. He is currently pursuing studies at the Indian Institute of Technology, Madras.

name: Manish Srivastava
role: CEO, AI CoE for Sustainable Cities (Airawat, IIT Kanpur)
html: Manish leads the AI Centre of Excellence for Sustainable Cities (Airawat) at IIT Kanpur. Before that he spent five years as CTO of eGov Foundation, building open digital infrastructure that lets millions access essential services across governance, finance, health, and sanitation, including the DIGIT platform underpinning PUCAR's court technology. His work sits at the intersection of cities, AI, governance, trust, and DPI.

name: Mehul Sehgal
html: Mehul is a Program Lead at PUCAR, which he joined after nearly four years at Samagra. There he helped set up the pilot 24x7 ON Court in Kerala, building digital public goods and improving legal processes around the court experience. He previously worked with KPMG and FICCI, and topped his class at Hindu College.

name: Muhammed Sawad P.M
html: Sawad is an L1 engineer on the support team behind PUCAR's DRISTI platform, based in Hyderabad.

name: Mohammed Anas Ahmed
html: Anas is an Assistant Manager at Flipkart working on inventory planning and control. He was earlier an Associate at Samagra, where he contributed to governance transformation programmes including PUCAR's justice work. He is a 2023 graduate of NIT Warangal.

name: Mugdha Mohapatra
html: Mugdha is a public policy researcher now pursuing her MPP in Berlin, where she also works with Democracy Reporting International. She spent two years as a researcher at XKDR Forum in Mumbai studying courts and governance reform, after a stint at the Centre for Policy Research. Her work combines law, qualitative research, and digital development.

name: Nandakumar C. K.
role: Senior Advocate, High Court of Karnataka
organisation: Law Chambers of C. K. Nandakumar
html: Nandakumar is a Senior Advocate at the High Court of Karnataka, designated in 2021, and a Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Arbitrators. He built the Law Chambers of C. K. Nandakumar over more than a decade into a strong corporate, commercial, and civil litigation and arbitration practice. He brings deep courtroom and arbitration experience to PUCAR's work.

name: Nikhil Nadiger
html: Nikhil was Vice President at Samagra, where as Programme Lead for PUCAR he led a 12-member team working with the judiciary in Karnataka and Kerala and the Supreme Court eCommittee to conceptualise, design, and build DRISTI, the open-source court technology behind filing, summons, hearings, and orders. He has since co-founded theStaffRoom, a platform and podcast for India's teachers.

name: Nitin Kashyap
html: Nitin was most recently Vice President of Products at Krutrim, building India's AI and compute ecosystem. Before that he was SVP and Head of Product at Samagra during its PUCAR engagement, following more than eight years at Google where he rose to Group Product Manager. He describes himself simply as dreamer, thinker, doer.

name: Pavithra Manivannan
html: Pavithra is Research Lead at XKDR Forum in Mumbai, where she has spent over five years working on court metrics, judicial reforms, and government contracting. Her research on how India's legal system actually performs feeds directly into PUCAR's measurement work. She earlier worked with the Finance Research Group.

name: Piyush Tewari
html: Piyush is the founder and CEO of SaveLIFE Foundation, which he started in 2008 and which secured India's Good Samaritan Law and the Motor Vehicles Amendment Act, 2019. Its work has cut road fatalities by half on corridors like the Mumbai-Pune Expressway. A Harvard MPA and 2024 Skoll Award winner, he brings hard-won experience of changing public systems from the outside.

name: Pramod Rao
html: Pramod served as Executive Director of SEBI until 2025, handling corporation finance and debt markets, with board roles at IFSCA, NFRA, and IICA. Earlier he was Group General Counsel at ICICI Bank and General Counsel for Citi South Asia. A lawyer, strategic advisor, and mentor, he has long championed innovation in how India resolves disputes.

name: Pramod Varma
role: Co-founder and Chief Architect, Networks for Humanity
html: Pramod is one of the architects of India's digital public infrastructure: former Chief Architect of Aadhaar and IndiaStack, co-creator of the Beckn protocol and Finternet, and long-time CTO of EkStep Foundation. He now co-founds Networks for Humanity and co-chairs the Centre for Digital Public Infrastructure. His thinking on open networks underpins how DRISTI is designed.

name: Priya Watwani
role: Human Rights Lawyer; MPP candidate, LSE
html: Priya is a human rights lawyer and an advocate with the Bar Council of Delhi since 2016. She spent over six years at iProbono, most recently as Senior Program Officer, connecting people who need legal help with lawyers willing to act pro bono. A Lady Meherbai D Tata Scholar, she is currently pursuing her Master of Public Policy at the London School of Economics.

name: Priyanka Yadav
html: Priyanka is a Director at Deloitte, leading sector-agnostic digital transformations built on open-source technology and digital public goods, with a focus on digital health. She previously worked at EY and volunteers across the Beckn open community, Sunbird RC, and the Centre for Digital Public Infrastructure. She brings DPI thinking into PUCAR's technology ecosystem.

name: Rahul Kulkarni
html: Rahul is co-founder and Chief Technologist of DoNew, a social enterprise started by ex-Googlers to make moonshot technology innovation possible for every organisation. He has spent the past decade helping public-purpose organisations in India rebuild themselves around frugal, ambitious technology, and now focuses on how organisations rebuild in the age of AI.

name: Rahul Matthan
html: Rahul is a Partner at Trilegal, where he has led the technology practice for over 25 years and helped shape much of India's technology law, from privacy and data protection to digital public infrastructure. He writes the widely read Ex Machina column on technology policy and is the author of books on privacy and DPI. His counsel anchors the legal architecture around PUCAR's open court technology.

name: Rajeesh Menon
html: Rajeesh is an entrepreneur and investor, currently building RubiX, an engine to curate and unpack experiences, and co-creator of the OneTAC Global Alliance, India's decentralised open network for tourism, arts, and culture built on Beckn. His work on open networks connects directly to the protocol thinking behind PUCAR's dispute resolution infrastructure.

name: Rajendran Nair Karakulam
html: Rajendran is the founder of Karakulam Consulting, a public policy and government relations firm helping businesses navigate India's regulatory and policy landscape. He was earlier Senior Manager for Policy at Presolv360, one of India's leading online dispute resolution platforms. He works from New Delhi at the intersection of policy and dispute resolution.

name: Rajneesh Jaswal
html: Rajneesh is co-founder, CEO, and board director of CADRE ODR, where he has spent seven years building what he calls a global dispute resolution operating system. He is also a Director of Equisolve Forum, the association of ODR providers in India's securities markets, and has taught public policy as visiting faculty at the National Law School of India University.

name: Ramu Kandimalla
html: Ramu is a Technical Project Manager at BeeHyv Software Solutions in Hyderabad, managing web and mobile application delivery for the engineering teams behind PUCAR's DRISTI platform. A certified Scrum Master, he focuses on project planning, resource management, and applying AI technologies to business applications.

name: Rangin Pallav Tripathy
role: Professor of Law, National Law University Odisha
html: Rangin is a Professor of Law at National Law University Odisha, where he has taught since 2010. A Fulbright Scholar at Harvard Law School, his research focuses on judicial accountability, transparency, and the empirical study of India's courts. He describes himself simply as a researcher and teacher.

name: Ravi Prakash
role: Head of Architecture and Technology Ecosystem, Beckn Labs
html: Ravi heads architecture and the technology ecosystem at Beckn Labs and is a genesis co-author of the Beckn protocol specification, the open network standard behind ONDC and other decentralised networks. He has spent seven years evolving the protocol from principal architect to ecosystem steward. The same open-network thinking shapes DRISTI's architecture.

name: Sarfraz Alam
html: Sarfraz is a Senior Software Engineer at Info Edge in Noida, working across the stack with Next.js and Node.js. A Google Summer of Code alumnus, he previously built at Helium and interned at Swiggy. He contributes engineering muscle to open-source projects, including PUCAR's technology.

name: Sarika Rastogi
role: Program Director, CSR Strategic Projects, Genpact
html: Sarika is a Program Director at Genpact leading CSR strategic projects, climate programmes, and sustainability reporting. She earlier directed the Genpact Centre for Process Transformation in collaboration with Piramal Foundation, and is a Master Black Belt in process transformation. A gold medalist in business economics, she is also a dedicated student of Iyengar yoga.

name: Satyam Thareja
html: Satyam is an Advocate-on-Record at the Supreme Court of India and founding partner of TNC in New Delhi. He earlier practised in the chambers of senior advocate Sidharth Luthra and served as law clerk and research assistant to Justice R. M. Lodha, former Chief Justice of India. He has practised law since 2012.

name: Sayak Mukherjee
html: Sayak is a software developer at Sama, India's leading online dispute resolution platform, where he has been building since 2021. He works remotely from Kolkata, with a background in data dashboards and full-stack development. His day job is quite literally building the future of dispute resolution.

name: Shiv Kumar Sareen
role: Former GM and Chief Compliance Officer, Bank of India
html: Shiv Kumar Sareen retired as General Manager and Chief Compliance Officer of Bank of India, then spent over eight years as Lead for Risk and Compliance at India Post Payments Bank, helping set up the greenfield bank's compliance and risk frameworks. He remains a subject matter expert on compliance with the Indian Institute of Banking and Finance.

name: Shraddha Paghdar
html: Shraddha is a Tech Lead and Senior Full Stack Developer at Sama, the online dispute resolution platform, where she has worked for four years. Before Sama she spent four years as a senior developer at Blackcurrant Apps in Mumbai. She also writes about technology as a freelance technical writer.

name: Shreyashi Soni
html: Shreyashi works with Samagra, the governance consulting firm whose justice portfolio supports PUCAR's court transformation work.

name: Shweta Devgan
role: Chief Compliance and Policy Officer, CORD
html: Shweta is the Chief Compliance and Policy Officer at CORD, where she has spent over six years aligning the organisation's dispute resolution products and processes with legal and policy requirements. An NLSIU Bangalore graduate, she focuses on equipping general counsels to resolve disputes digitally, including through TERES. She works remotely from Himachal Pradesh.

name: Siddarth Raman
role: CTO, The Professeer; Researcher, XKDR Forum
html: Siddarth is the Chief Technology Officer of The Professeer and a researcher at XKDR Forum, where he has studied courts and legal system performance since 2023. He was earlier an independent consultant working with Bain & Co, Callan Consulting, and NGOs across technology, government, and the social sector, and volunteered with Mumbai's municipal corporation during the pandemic.

name: Kuncharam Sravani Reddy
html: Sravani is a Consultant at Deloitte, where she has worked since 2022 on project implementation. Her unusual path runs from practising dentistry in Telangana through hospital administration to technology consulting. She supports the delivery side of PUCAR's ecosystem work.

name: Subbaiah K G
html: Subbaiah is Head of Product and a board director at CADRE ODR. He brings over a decade of consumer product experience, having been Head of Products at Nestaway and a product manager at Ola and Goodbox. At CADRE he applies that product discipline to making dispute resolution work at scale.

name: Subhashini Srinivasan
html: Subhashini is a Senior Technical Architect at eGov Foundation, architecting digital public goods platforms at population scale, including the technology behind PUCAR's court work. She spent eight years as software lead for the Karnataka Learning Partnership at Akshara Foundation, building open-source tools to visualise public education data. She is an expert in national-scale open-source platforms for public service delivery.

name: Supriya Sankaran
html: Supriya is the co-founder of Agami and of PUCAR itself, working to transform how every Indian experiences justice. Through the Agami Prize, Agami Challenges, and AgamiShaala she has spent years unleashing changemakers in law and justice. Before Agami she was a Director at Ashoka Innovators for the Public, and she started out as a corporate lawyer.

name: Suresh Unnikrishnan
html: Suresh is a Vice President at Samagra, where he leads engineering on digital public goods, including the DRISTI platform behind PUCAR's 24x7 ON Courts. He was earlier Head of Engineering at First WalkIn Technologies for seven years and co-founded LivinCloud, a customer engagement platform. His career spans SaaS, startups, and engineering leadership.

name: Surya Prakash B S
html: Surya Prakash is Fellow and Programme Director at DAKSH, where he has spent a decade leading the Rule of Law Project, using quantitative research to map case pendency and the administration of justice in India. A chartered accountant and lawyer by background, he previously led tax controversy work at IBM across Asia-Pacific. DAKSH's data work is foundational to how PUCAR measures court performance.

name: Susan Thomas
html: Susan is a Senior Fellow at XKDR Forum and one of India's most respected empirical economists. She spent nearly 25 years as a professor at IGIDR Bombay, and her research on markets, firms, and the legal system underpins much of the evidence base for judicial reform. At XKDR she leads work measuring how India's courts actually perform.

name: Susmita Behera
html: Susmita is a Technical Project Manager at BeeHyv, where she has managed large-scale programmes including national health campaigns across Nigeria reaching millions of beneficiaries. She holds PMP, PRINCE2, Lean Six Sigma, and SAFe certifications, with earlier stints at Finastra. She manages delivery for the engineering teams behind PUCAR's DRISTI platform.

name: Swathi Sukumar
html: Swathi is a Senior Advocate at the Delhi High Court, designated in 2024, and one of India's leading intellectual property litigators. She is co-founder of iProbono India, which has connected thousands of people to pro bono legal help since 2010, and a door tenant at Hogarth Chambers in London. Her TEDx talk asks why lawyers in India should do pro bono work.

name: Tahera Bharmal
html: Tahera is a Product Manager at eGov Foundation, building open-source digital public infrastructure that solves societal problems at scale, from sanitation systems to PUCAR's court technology. A Young India Fellow, she works on making complex multi-stakeholder systems coordinate seamlessly.

name: Tanmay Bhateja
html: Tanmay is a Senior Associate at Samagra, which he joined in 2023 after graduating from Shaheed Sukhdev College of Business Studies. At Samagra he works on governance transformation programmes, including the justice portfolio supporting PUCAR's 24x7 ON Courts.

name: Trishal Kumar
html: Trishal is a Senior Consultant in Deloitte's Government and Public Services practice in Bengaluru, where he has worked since 2022. He supports large public sector transformation programmes, including work connected to PUCAR's court technology ecosystem.

name: Unmesh Kulkarni
html: Unmesh is a founder member of Oceanic Circles and serves on the advisory board of IIT Indore's School of Innovation, where he is an honorary Professor of Practice. A veteran technology leader from Pune, he is currently building a new venture. He brings decades of innovation and mentoring experience to the collective.

name: Vamsi Madhav
html: Vamsi is the CEO of Finvu, one of India's licensed Account Aggregators, and was earlier COO of Sahamati, the foundation driving open banking with the account aggregator framework. He has spent years building the consent-based data-sharing rails of India's fintech stack, experience that maps directly onto consent flows in justice infrastructure.

name: Varun Basu
role: VP, Growth and Partnerships, eGov Foundation
html: Varun is Vice President for Growth and Partnerships at eGov Foundation, driving adoption of digital public infrastructure for social impact. He was earlier Head of Global Sales at LitmusWorld and co-founded Getkrrackin. At eGov he builds the partner ecosystem around platforms like DIGIT and the court technology behind PUCAR.

name: Rajesh Cherukumalli
html: Rajesh is a Lead QA Engineer at BeeHyv in Hyderabad, with over a decade in software quality across Accenture and Caliber Technologies. A certified software quality engineer, he leads testing for the engineering teams behind PUCAR's DRISTI platform.

name: Vijaykarthick T
role: Senior Key Accounts Manager, Shriram Life Insurance
html: Vijaykarthick is a Senior Key Accounts Manager at Shriram Life Insurance in Chennai. He was earlier Sales and Marketing Manager at JustAct, the online dispute resolution platform, and spent over a decade in account management in Dubai. He brings a market-facing view of how dispute resolution services reach users.

name: Vikas Mahendra
role: Co-founder, CORD; Joint Secretary, Arbitration Bar of India
html: Vikas is a co-founder of CORD (Centre for Online Resolution of Disputes) and Joint Secretary of the Arbitration Bar of India, now building AI for complex disputes. A construction disputes specialist, he spent six years at Herbert Smith Freehills across London, Paris, and Singapore, and nine years as Partner at Keystone Partners in Bengaluru. He also advises TERES and co-chairs the Campaign for Greener Arbitrations.

name: Vinay Mahendra
html: Vinay is a co-founder of CORD, the Centre for Online Dispute Resolution, where he has built legal-tech products like TERES and Arbitraverse since 2019. Before CORD he spent over a decade in risk and finance technology across TCS, HCL, and CGI in the US and Canada. He pairs financial-systems rigour with legal-tech ambition.

name: Vishwam Jindal
html: Vishwam is the CEO of Webnyay, building generative AI solutions for the legal sector. A former dispute resolution and investigations solicitor at DLA Piper and Linklaters, he now serves on CII's National Legal Services Committee, co-chairs the Campaign for Greener Arbitrations, and teaches as adjunct faculty at NALSAR University of Law.

name: Yashna Jhamb
html: Yashna is co-founder of Dots, the Ooloi Labs spin-off that turns scattered user feedback into structured, traceable insights. She co-founded Ooloi Labs in 2019 and is an Acumen India Fellow focused on driving data-driven impact in social systems.
